plugin_desc: Benu Networks router SNMP child template (enterprise PEN 39406). Beyond the shared Router SNMP baseline for MIB-II uptime and IF-MIB interface traffic, it collects BENU-CHASSIS-MIB sensor temperature, fan status and power-supply status. Host software task CPU and memory tables are process-level metrics and are not promoted to shared device CPU or memory health metrics here.
name: Router

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: Highest Benu chassis temperature sensor value in degrees Celsius, using BENU-CHASSIS-MIB sensor rows whose type is temperature.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: Worst Benu fan sensor state, normalized from benuSensorState so normal(1) is healthy and every other state is abnormal.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: Worst Benu power-supply status, normalized from present and powered states so present and powered supply rows are healthy and every other state is abnormal.

metrics.name: device_psu_powered
metrics.description: This metric indicates whether the device PSU is powered on (1) or off (2).

metrics.name: device_psu_present
metrics.description: This metric indicates whether the device PSU slot is populated (1) or empty (2).

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCInOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, based on the 64-bit ifHCOutOctets counter which does not wrap on high-speed interfaces.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
